meaning: n.悲痛,穷困,不幸,危难,忧伤v.使悲痛,使穷困,使忧伤
examples: She was obviously in distress after the attack.
Widows seem to experience more distress than widowers.
He keeps coming to the aid of this damsel in distress.
Your counsel says that you wished to avoid causing further distress to Tia's family.
So why ignore a figure capturing VeriSign's substantial distress, which will continue to affect the company?
